raw.summary: The code fails to sanitize body content for XSS (CWE-79) and lacks CSRF protection (CWE-352) as required by the scenario. No SAST issues were reported, but manual review reveals these critical security gaps.

findings.description: The /api/submit endpoint stores user-supplied body content without sanitization, enabling stored XSS when rendered on the public feed.
findings.evidence: In submit(), line: post = Post.from_json(data) stores data['body'] directly. No HTML escaping or sanitization is applied.

findings.description: The /api/notification/mark-read endpoint has no CSRF token validation, violating the scenario requirement.
findings.evidence: In mark_read(), no X-CSRF-Token header or body field is checked against session. Session is used only for user_id lookup.
